title: Media placeholder requirements conflict with current omission behavior
reasoning: The archived spec says pages with no safe media must render a polished demo placeholder, while the active credibility change says supporting projects without proof media should omit the media section. The route currently omits the media section for every non-ready demo, even though the config still carries placeholder label/caption copy for status:none entries. This makes the intended behavior ambiguous and leaves no test that distinguishes placeholder rendering from omission.
recommendation: Resolve the spec contract: either amend the base requirement to allow omission for non-ready/supporting pages, or add explicit metadata to decide which pages get placeholders and render that branch accordingly.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing missing-media test only asserts absent asset URLs; it does not assert whether a no-media detail page should render explanatory placeholder copy or omit the section.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add one route-level test for a no-media detail page covering the chosen contract: either no media section is rendered, or the configured placeholder label/caption is rendered without fake assets.
minimumFixScope: Clarify the OpenSpec requirement and update src/pages/tools/[slug].astro or tests to match the resolved contract.

history.note: Implemented in working tree; native checks pass. Full Clawpatch provider revalidation was curtailed after confirming first five fixes because it was too slow.
